source_jobs.title: Insurance Billing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
About Wisdom

Wisdom blends industry expertise with advanced technology to make dental practices work better for everyone involved. We believe dentistry is about people, and we exist to make the future of dentistry stronger and more sustainable for dentists, their teams, and the patients they serve. We match administrative teams with expert billers and custom-built technology to take on the heavy lifting of dental billing while maximizing dentists’ time in-office, and their bottom line.



Coming from a fresh $21M Series A round of funding we are looking for exceptional candidates to help us build a category-defining company. Wisdom has employees across the US.



About The Role

Our Insurance Billing Specialists focus on keeping insurance billing moving by submitting claims, posting dental insurance payments, and working insurance aging reports for our customers. This work is at the heart of Wisdom’s service offerings, and is a large part of what allows us to provide outstanding services to the dental offices we serve. As an Insurance Billing Specialist, you’ll:



 - Prepare and submit dental insurance claims promptly and accurately, following up as necessary to ensure prompt payment and resolve any issues or discrepancies with insurance companies

 - Post insurance payments and adjustments to patient accounts, reconciling insurance payments with the PMS and investigating any discrepancies

 - Monitor and manage accounts receivable, ensuring timely collection of outstanding insurance balances and running regular reports on AR to identify trends and areas for improvement

 - Partner directly with offices and insurance companies, acting as their primary point of contact for any insurance-related inquiries and regularly communicating challenges and successes

 - Coordinate with dental offices to ensure accurate coding and documentation for all insurance claims



Why Wisdom?

 - Work remotely alongside a fully remote team that knows how to get stuff done, without the pain and drama of in-office work.

 - Flexible hours

 - Support and inclusion no matter your background. Whether you’re a seasoned remote biller or you’re testing the waters for the first time, we’ll set you up with the tools, training, and community support you need to succeed at Wisdom.

 - A better experience for billers. We’re building tools and leveraging technology to save you time and let you focus on earning more, faster.



We’d Love to Hear From You If You Have

 - At least 5 years of experience in dental insurance claim submission, claim posting, and AR management

 - Must have a minimum of 8 hours per week of availability during standard business hours (Monday–Friday, 8am–5pm CST)

 - Strong knowledge of dental insurance plans, procedures, and coding

 - Exceptional problem-solving skills and the ability to handle complex billing issues with care and a commitment to patient confidentiality and data security

 - Excellent communication, interpersonal, and follow-up skills

 - Proficiency in dental practice management software (e.g., Dentrix, Eaglesoft) and Google Workspaces



Wisdom is an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Insurance Verification Specialist
source_jobs.description:
About Wisdom

Wisdom blends industry expertise with advanced technology to make dental practices work better for everyone involved. We believe dentistry is about people, and we exist to make the future of dentistry stronger and more sustainable for dentists, their teams, and the patients they serve. We match administrative teams with expert billers and custom-built technology to take on the heavy lifting of dental billing while maximizing dentists’ time in-office, and their bottom line.



Coming from a fresh $21M Series A round of funding we are looking for exceptional candidates to help us build a category-defining company. Wisdom has employees across the US.



About The Role

Our Insurance Verification Specialists focus on ensuring the accuracy and efficiency of dental insurance verification processes for our customers. Insurance Verification is a crucial step in setting up an office for success with their billing needs and is the first step of revenue cycle management. Having the most complete and up-to-date information in the patient insurance file is necessary for clean claim submissions and proper treatment planning of procedures and over-the-counter collections.



As an Insurance Verification Specialist, you’ll:

 - Complete insurance eligibility verifications by phone, web, and fax, tracking and summarizing your work so that every office is well-informed of our progress

 - Obtain and enter Full Insurance Breakdowns into our client practice management systems

 - Partner directly with our customers to make sure verifications get done, highlighting failed verifications to offices and requesting additional information as needed

 - Maintaining constant timeliness and accuracy

 - Complete and transmit invoicing forms monthly



Why Wisdom?

 - Work remotely alongside a fully remote team that knows how to get stuff done, without the pain and drama of in-office work.

 - Support and inclusion no matter your background. Whether you’re a seasoned remote biller or you’re testing the waters for the first time, we’ll set you up with the tools, training, and community support you need to succeed at Wisdom.

 - A better experience for billers. We’re building tools and leveraging technology to save you time and let you focus on earning more, faster.



We’d Love to Hear From You If You Have

 - Strong knowledge of dental insurance policies, procedures, and industry trends; experience in dental insurance verification preferred, with a minimum of 2 years of dental office experience, preferably in an admin role.

 - Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal

 - Exceptional attention to detail and organizational abilities

 - Familiarity with a variety of practice management solutions (PMS) is highly regarded

 - Knowledge of, and ability to comply with, HIPAA, HITECH, and patient confidentiality policies

 - Proficiency in Google Suite and Microsoft Office



Wisdom is an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Operations Team Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Wisdom

Wisdom blends industry expertise with advanced technology to make dental practices work better for everyone involved. We believe dentistry is about people, and we exist to make the future of dentistry stronger and more sustainable for dentists, their teams, and the patients they serve. We match administrative teams with expert billers and custom-built technology to take on the heavy lifting of dental billing while maximizing dentists’ time in-office, and their bottom line.



Coming from a fresh $21M Series A round of funding we are looking for exceptional candidates to help us build a category-defining company. Wisdom has employees across the US.



Do you love leading people, improving processes, and driving real results? We’re looking for an Operations Team Lead to help guide our dental billing operations, coach a high-performing team, and deliver standout service across a portfolio of 35–40 clients.

What you’ll do:

 - Lead, mentor, and hold teams accountable to performance goals, with a strong focus on KPIs, quality, and client retention.

 - Own the client experience by driving clear communication, leading quality business reviews, and resolving issues quickly and thoughtfully.

 - Identify opportunities to streamline workflows, improve efficiency, and help the operation scale smarter, not harder.
   
   

Requirements:

 - 2 years of leadership experience

 - 5 years of dental experience with virtual experience preferred
   

What we offer:

 - Fully remote, U.S.-based W2 role

 - Competitive base salary

 - Unlimited Paid Time Off

 - Comprehensive benefits including health, dental, vision, 401k, and HSA

 - Company-provided computer and annual in-person leadership events
   

If you’re a strategic thinker, decisive communicator, and tech-savvy leader who knows how to bring out the best in people, we’d love to connect.



See job description: Operations Team Lead to join the fully remote team at Wisdom!

#OperationsTeamLead #RemoteJobs #DentalBilling #Leadership #FinTech #Hiring



Wisdom is an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Wisdom

Wisdom blends industry expertise with advanced technology to make dental practices work better for everyone involved. We believe dentistry is about people, and we exist to make the future of dentistry stronger and more sustainable for dentists, their teams, and the patients they serve. We match administrative teams with expert billers and custom-built technology to take on the heavy lifting of dental billing while maximizing dentists’ time in-office, and their bottom line.



Coming from a fresh $21M Series A round of funding we are looking for exceptional candidates to help us build a category-defining company. Wisdom has employees across the US.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The roadmap isn't handed to you here. You'll help write it.

As a Senior Software Engineer at Wisdom, you'll own hard problems end-to-end — from schema design to agentic billing pipelines to the React surfaces that billers and practice operators rely on every day. You'll work on a small, high-trust team where your technical decisions have real consequences, the feedback loop with users is short, and your fingerprints are on the product.

Wisdom's stack is TypeScript, Node.js, React, Postgres, and AWS. The problems we're solving — reliable integrations with inconsistent insurance systems, AI-driven billing decisions that have to be right, HIPAA-compliant infrastructure that can't fail — are legitimately hard. We'd rather have someone energized by that than simply tolerant of it.

In your first year, you'll have shipped core pieces of our agentic billing infrastructure, established deep ownership over at least one major product surface, and helped raise the bar for how this team builds. This is a fully remote role reporting directly to the Head of Engineering.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Design and ship production-grade features across the full stack — backend services, cloud infrastructure, and user-facing React interfaces

 - Build and evolve AI-powered agentic workflows that automate complex, high-stakes billing decisions at scale — not prototypes, production systems

 - Architect resilient integrations with dental insurance carriers and practice management systems (Dentrix, Eaglesoft) — poorly documented, inconsistent, and absolutely critical to get right

 - Work directly with dental billers and practice operators to understand problems at ground level, then translate that into software decisions

 - Hold a high bar for code quality, reliability, and operational health across the codebase — including code review and technical documentation that actually gets used

 - Take on ambiguous, undefined problems and drive them to resolution without waiting for permission or a perfect brief


WHO YOU ARE

 - 5+ years shipping production software in environments where reliability and quality had real consequences — not just feature velocity

 - You've built systems under pressure: integrations that had to stay up, services that had to scale, and code that had to be owned and maintained beyond your tenure

 - Hands-on production experience with at least one major LLM API — OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), or Google Vertex AI — including tool use, prompt architecture, structured outputs, and handling model behavior in live systems

 - Strong command of TypeScript/JavaScript; familiarity with Python or C# is a plus

 - Deep experience with relational databases — schema design, query optimization, and reasoning about data integrity under load

 - Cloud infrastructure experience in AWS or GCP, including deploying and operating services at production scale

 - You default to ownership and move toward hard problems rather than away from them

 - You're direct, intellectually honest, and collaborative — you surface bad news early, change your mind when the evidence warrants it, and make the people around you sharper


YOU'LL STAND OUT IF YOU HAVE

 - Experience with the Mastra agent framework or comparable orchestration tools (LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI)

 - Working know

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About Wisdom

Wisdom blends industry expertise with advanced technology to make dental practices work better for everyone involved. We believe dentistry is about people, and we exist to make the future of dentistry stronger and more sustainable for dentists, their teams, and the patients they serve. We match administrative teams with expert billers and custom-built technology to take on the heavy lifting of dental billing while maximizing dentists’ time in-office, and their bottom line.



Coming from a fresh $21M Series A round of funding we are looking for exceptional candidates to help us build a category-defining company. Wisdom has employees across the US.



About The Role

Wisdom is seeking outbound Sales Development Representatives (SDRs) to join our growing sales team. In this role, you’ll engage dentists and office managers across multiple channels including phone, email, and social to schedule meetings for our Account Executives. You'll have the opportunity to travel to conferences across the country to find new leads, and will help the sales department reach their revenue goals.



This is a high-impact, business critical role that will report into the Sales Manager. This role is in person, based in Lehi, UT.



What You’ll Do

 - Outreach to a list of dental offices via phone, email, and social media to find interested prospects and schedule demos for the Account Executive team

 - Attend dental industry conferences and events to find additional leads

 - Nurture and manage each lead through to a completed demo

 - Leverage Gong and Hubspot to track and log progress on leads

 - Collaborate with the broader sales team to ensure a smooth customer journey



We’d Love To Hear From You If You Have

 - Experience in sales, recruiting, or a similar outbound role or are a recent graduate eager to start a sales career

 - A track record of successfully meeting and exceeding goals

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - An ability to manage a high volume of tasks in an organized way

 - A growth mindset paired with a desire to receive and implement coaching and feedback

 - Healthcare or dental specific experience preferred, but not required



What we’ll provide

 - Unlimited paid time off

 - Competitive medical, dental, and vision coverage

 - 401k plan

 - Paid Parental Leave

 - Fast career growth opportunities for top performers



Wisdom is an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager (AI)
source_jobs.description:
About Wisdom

Wisdom blends industry expertise with advanced technology to make dental practices work better for everyone involved. We believe dentistry is about people, and we exist to make the future of dentistry stronger and more sustainable for dentists, their teams, and the patients they serve. We match administrative teams with expert billers and custom-built technology to take on the heavy lifting of dental billing while maximizing dentists’ time in-office, and their bottom line.



Coming from a fresh $21M Series A round of funding we are looking for exceptional candidates to help us build a category-defining company. Wisdom has employees across the US.



The Role: Principal Product Manager (AI)

Dental insurance billing is one of the largest manual workflows in US healthcare. Claims aging, appeals, posting, follow-ups makes for work that today eats entire FTE teams at every dental practice. The expertise that makes it run lives in the heads of veteran dental billers. We're building AI agents that work alongside them and support them through these workflows.

We're hiring a Principal PM to own a major surface of this. Small pods, all builders, reporting to the Head of Product. You'll help shape Wisdom's product strategy for the next 1-3 years.

What You'll Do

 - Own strategy and roadmap for an AI-first product. Balance short-term wins (human-assisted tools) with long-term bets (autonomous AI agents).

 - Shadow revenue specialists, billers, and dental office managers to understand workflows end-to-end. Translate tribal knowledge from 10+ year industry veterans into structured product workflows and context for AI.

 - Be on the forefront of AI design patterns. Help us build new agentic workflows, human-in-the-loop UX patterns, AI-to-human escalation paths, and audit trails.

 - Define gradual autonomy models: when should AI act autonomously vs. escalate to humans.

What We're Looking For

 - 8+ years as a Product Manager, with at least 3 years working on AI/ML products.

 - You're already shipping AI products. You've built things with LLMs and agent frameworks, you've felt the pain of eval infra and prompt regressions, and you have strong opinions on the mechanics of how we build.

 - You leverage AI across the entire stack of the job: drafting and pressure-testing strategy, running synthetic user research, building evals before specs, prototyping flows in code, and shipping PRs when it's faster than writing them up. You treat AI like a co-worker you're constantly delegating to and learning from.

 - You've built pure agentic or human-in-the-loop systems before. You can make tradeoffs between "AI decides, AI performs," "AI suggests, human approves," "AI acts, human audits," or even "AI skipped, human only." Bonus if you've done this in healthcare, fintech, or another regulated space where being wrong has massive consequences.



Wisdom is an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Inbound Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About Wisdom

Wisdom blends industry expertise with advanced technology to make dental practices work better for everyone involved. We believe dentistry is about people, and we exist to make the future of dentistry stronger and more sustainable for dentists, their teams, and the patients they serve. We match administrative teams with expert billers and custom-built technology to take on the heavy lifting of dental billing while maximizing dentists’ time in-office, and their bottom line.



Coming from a fresh $21M Series A round of funding we are looking for exceptional candidates to help us build a category-defining company. Wisdom has employees across the US.



About The Role

Wisdom is seeking a full cycle Account Executive to join our growing sales team. In this role, you’ll outreach via inbound channels to schedule your own meetings, pitch dental practices on Wisdom’s service offerings, and close business. You’ll also upsell clients on add-on services to expand their scope over time.



This is a high-impact, business critical role that will report into the Sales Manager, Jack Seal. This role is fully remote and can be based anywhere in the US.



What You’ll Do

 - Build and manage a sales pipeline of independent dental offices and small group practices through inbound inquiries, paid marketing, and referrals

 - Schedule your own sales meetings in order to convert leads into qualified deals

 - Conduct sales meetings with Office Managers and Dentists

 - Progress deals through late stage pipeline to close, running scoping calls with subject matter experts as needed

 - Upsell additional services to existing customers to growth their portfolio of services

 - Leverage Gong and Hubspot to track and log progress on leads and deals

 - Partner with our marketing, onboarding, and delivery teams to ensure a seamless customer journey from first impression to go-live

 - Attend dental industry conferences and events locally and nationwide



We’d Love To Hear From You If You Have

 - At least 5 years of full-cycle sales experience or a combination of Sales Development Representative and Closing experience

 - Experience selling to SMB customers, dental industry preferred

 - A track record of successfully meeting and exceeding quotas

 - An ability to manage a high volume of sales tasks in an organized way using a CRM, Hubspot experience preferred

 - A growth mindset paired with a desire to receive and implement coaching and feedback



What we’ll provide

 - Unlimited paid time off

 - Competitive medical, dental, and vision coverage

 - Remote-first culture

 - 401k plan

 - Paid Parental Leave



Wisdom is an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected status in accordance with applicable law.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
About Wisdom

Wisdom blends industry expertise with advanced technology to make dental practices work better for everyone involved. We believe dentistry is about people, and we exist to make the future of dentistry stronger and more sustainable for dentists, their teams, and the patients they serve. We match administrative teams with expert billers and custom-built technology to take on the heavy lifting of dental billing while maximizing dentists’ time in-office, and their bottom line.



Coming from a fresh $21M Series A round of funding we are looking for exceptional candidates to help us build a category-defining company. Wisdom has employees across the US.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The roadmap isn't handed to you here. You'll help write it — and you'll be the reason it stays up.



As a Staff Software Engineer focused on Infrastructure at Wisdom, you'll set the technical direction for reliability across the company — and own the systems behind the systems: the deploy pipeline, the observability, the capacity controls, and the failure-handling that decide whether our agentic billing infrastructure quietly does its job or pages someone at 2am. This is a force-multiplier role on a small, high-trust team. Your job isn't just to fix what breaks; it's to make the whole organization operate at a higher reliability bar — to build the practices, the guardrails, and the instincts that mean fewer things break in the first place, and the team can handle the ones that do without you in the room.



Wisdom's stack is TypeScript, Node.js, React, Postgres, and AWS, with LLM-driven agents (Mastra, Anthropic) making high-stakes billing decisions in production. The problems we're solving — keeping inconsistent insurance integrations alive, making AI pipelines fail safe instead of failing loud, running HIPAA-compliant infrastructure that genuinely can't go down — are legitimately hard. We'd rather have someone energized by making things not break than someone who merely tolerates being paged when they do.



In your first year, you'll have defined what reliability means at Wisdom and built the function to deliver it: a real observability and SLO practice, an incident process that runs without heroics, agentic pipelines that degrade gracefully instead of taking prod down with them, and a team that's measurably better at operating production because of how you've raised the bar. This is a fully remote role reporting directly to the Head of Engineering.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Set the reliability strategy for the platform — SLOs, error budgets, and the operating standards for services that bill real money for real practices, and the technical roadmap to get us there

 - Own observability end-to-end — tracing, metrics, logging, and alerting (Datadog) that surfaces problems before users do, not after — and make it the default so any engineer can lead an incident, not just the person who wrote the code

 - Define how we operate AI-powered agentic workflows at scale — retries and backpressure, idempotency, graceful degradation, and capacity controls for LLM-driven pipelines. The failure modes here are new (batch blowups, stream drops, runaway cost, model misbehavior); you'll be writing the playbook the rest of the industry hasn't written yet, and setting the patterns the team builds against

 - Harden the integration surface with dental insurance carriers and practice management systems (Dentrix, Eaglesoft) — poorly documented, inconsistent, and the first thing to buckle under load

 - Own deploy and release engineering — fast, safe, reversible deploys; infrastructure as code (Terraform); and the unglamorous discipline that lets a Series A ship many times a day without breaking things

 - Build the incident practice, not just lead incidents — the on-call rotation, the runbooks, the blameless post-incident culture, and the follow-up discipline that turns outages into permanent fixes the whole team owns

 - Raise the bar through others — set technical stan

notes: Dental billing and technology company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles.
